reasoning: The document shows significant content loss on Slide 3, where detailed nested list items (over 75% of that slide's text) were omitted. This is strongly corroborated by the very low shingling recall score (0.404), indicating that a large portion of the source text is missing from the output. Additionally, the loss of functional status indicators (checkmark icons) and key URLs makes the document substantively incomplete.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The output contains a major omission of content in Section 3 (Page 3). The source slide features detailed nested bullet points under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' categories. The JSON output fails to capture any of these sub-points (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Elevation download', 'Website redesign'), losing more than 75% of the text for that slide. Additionally, the URL 'data.nconemap.com' is omitted from Slide 2.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 3 lists multiple sub-points: Infrastructure buildout (Replicate data between web servers, Replicate all map/image services between data centers, Replicate geodatabases between DB servers), EPA parcels project implementation, Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab, Website redesign/integration (NC OneMap, Geoportal).

concerns.analysis: On page 3, the list conversion is severely incomplete. The source contains a complex nested list structure under 'Ongoing' (including 'Infrastructure buildout' with three sub-items and 'EPA parcels project implementation') and under 'Upcoming' (including 'Elevation download...' and 'Website redesign...' with two sub-items). The JSON output only captures the top-level categories 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming', omitting the entire substantive list of tasks. This is a major failure of structural fidelity
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 3 list content including: 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data between web servers', 'Replicate all map/image services...', 'Replicate geodatabases...', 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Elevation download...', 'Website redesign/integration', 'NC OneMap', 'Geoportal'.

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the 'Tasks' section is significantly different and incomplete in the HTML output. In the source PDF (page 3), the 'Tasks' section includes a detailed hierarchy of sub-bullets under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' headings, including specific tasks like 'Infrastructure buildout' and 'Website redesign/integration'. In the HTML rendering, only the top-level 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' bullets are present; all the nested content is missing. This fundamentally changes the visual flow

concerns.output_quote: The HTML 'Tasks' section only contains two bullets: 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming'. All sub-bullets are missing.
concerns.location: Tasks section, following slide 3 of source.

concerns.analysis: The source PDF uses green checkmark icons on page 3 as meaningful status indicators for specific sub-tasks. In the HTML output, these icons are mostly missing. Only one large green checkmark icon appears, but it is misplaced at the bottom of the section and not associated with any specific text. Additionally, the URL text 'data.nconemap.com' which appeared on slide 2 is visually absent from the HTML rendering after the 'Geoportal Website:' label.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 3 shows three green checkmark icons next to completed tasks. Slide 2 includes the URL 'data.nconemap.com'.
concerns.output_quote: One large green checkmark icon is rendered at the bottom of the Tasks section without context. The URL text is missing from the 'Data Releases' section.

concerns.analysis: On page 2, the sidebar includes the URL 'data.nconemap.com'. The output JSON identifies the label 'Geoportal Website:' but completely omits the actual URL string. This is a missing link/URL issue.

action_items: Manually restore the nested list content on Slide 3
Re-insert the missing URL data.nconemap.com on Slide 2
Ensure the meaning of the status icons (green checkmarks) is captured as text or accessible images
